items.definition_en: A lawn is an area of short grass in a garden.
items.example_en: Children are playing on the lawn.

items.definition_en: A pond is a small area of water in a garden.
items.example_en: There are fish swimming in the pond.

items.definition_en: A path is a small road or walkway in a garden.
items.example_en: They walked down the path to the shed.

items.definition_en: A greenhouse is a glass building used to grow plants.
items.example_en: Tom keeps his tomatoes in the greenhouse.

items.definition_en: A hedge is a row of bushes that marks a garden area.
items.example_en: The hedge needs to be trimmed soon.

items.definition_en: An orchard is an area with many fruit trees.
items.example_en: We picked apples in the orchard.

items.definition_en: A rockery is a garden area with rocks and special plants.
items.example_en: She added more stones to the rockery.

items.definition_en: A terrace is a flat open area next to a house where you can sit.
items.example_en: We drink coffee on the terrace every morning.

items.definition_en: An arbour is a shaded garden area with climbing plants.
items.example_en: The arbour is covered in roses.

items.definition_en: A driveway is a path for cars at the front of a house or garden.
items.example_en: There is a car parked in the driveway.

items.definition_en: A shed is a small building used for storing garden tools.
items.example_en: He keeps the lawn mower in the shed.

items.definition_en: A pergola is a garden structure with open sides and climbing plants.
items.example_en: The pergola gives some shade on sunny days.

items.definition_en: Compost is a mixture of garden waste that turns into soil.
items.example_en: Organic waste goes into the compost.

items.definition_en: A courtyard is an open space surrounded by walls or buildings.
items.example_en: The courtyard is a quiet place to relax in summer.
